title: Surf Bus or Van Loft Bed
summary: A loft bed built and decorated to look like a classic VW-style surf bus, complete with plywood wheels, a front grill, headlights, and a ladder.
description:
Under the decoration, this is a straightforward 2x4 loft bed platform on four legs — the surf-bus look comes entirely from what gets wrapped around it afterward: plywood side panels shaped and detailed to read as a van, round wheels that can be made to spin on a single bolt, a built-up grill, and craft-store or shop-cut headlight rounds. Building the structural platform and legs first, and treating the decorative shell as its own separate phase, keeps the project from feeling overwhelming — get the bed itself square, sturdy, and at the right height before worrying about a single wheel or headlight.

The upper rail trim pieces are all cut to fit rather than to a fixed number, since small variations in your framing will change their exact lengths — measure your own assembled platform before cutting these. The 4" lag bolts holding the bed deck to the legs are worth using over plain screws if you ever want to break the bed down again; 3" screws work fine if you don't plan to disassemble it.

tools.note: Cuts the wheels, headlight rounds, and the ladder's rounded top edge.

materials.name: 1/4" to 1/2" plywood, paneling, or MDF, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Decorative cover for the bus body.

materials.name: Lag bolts, 4"
materials.note: Attaches the bed deck to the legs so it stays removable; 3" screws work if you don't need to disassemble it.

materials.name: Plywood scrap
materials.note: Leftover offcuts from the sheet goods above, for the wheels.

materials.name: Plywood scrap or craft-store precut round

cutList.note: Cut to fit your assembled platform.
Shortest-point measurement; bottom end cut at 5° off square.

cutList.material: Plywood scrap or craft-store precut round
Plywood scrap or craft-store precut round

cutList.note: Cut to fit the front and back of the bed.

steps.title: Frame the bed platform
steps.body: Frame the bed platform with the 2x4 frame ends, sides, and center joists. Use the 1-1/2" pocket-hole setting to drill your holes, and attach with 2-1/2" pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Deck the platform
steps.body: Attach the plywood or MDF deck to the top of the platform framing. Use either glue and 1-1/4" finish nails, or 2" wood screws.
materials: 1/4" to 1/2" plywood, paneling, or MDF, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Build the legs
steps.body: Build four legs from the leg verticals and horizontals, following your reference diagram, using 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Set the bed height and attach the platform
steps.body: Mark each leg set at the height you want the finished bed. Attach the bed platform to the legs from the inside with 2-1/2" screws or the 4" lag bolts — lag bolts keep the whole bed removable later if you ever need to break it down.

steps.title: Attach the end rails
steps.body: Attach the end rails to the legs and side rails with 2" wood screws.

steps.title: Attach the bottom supports
steps.body: Drill 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the 2x4 bottom supports and attach with 2-1/2" pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Cover the base
steps.body: Cover the base of the bed in plywood or MDF. Use 2" wood screws if you want the pieces easy to disassemble later, or nails and glue for a permanent build.
materials: 1/4" to 1/2" plywood, paneling, or MDF, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Cut and attach the wheels
steps.body: Cut the wheels from scrap plywood and attach them to the side panels. Use a single bolt through the center of each if you'd like the wheels to spin.

steps.title: Build the grill, bumpers, and headlights
steps.body: Cut the bumpers to fit the front and back of the bed. Build the grill from the uprights and crosspieces using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws, then attach it to the front with finish nails and glue. Cut the headlights as 4" and 6" rounds from plywood, or use precut wood rounds from a craft store.
materials: Plywood scrap or craft-store precut round

steps.title: Build and attach the ladder
steps.body: Attach the rungs to the underside of the ladder rails with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws, and glue. Round the top edge of the rails with a jigsaw. Attach the finished ladder to the inside of the bed's 2x4 legs with screws.

images.alt: Loft bed built and decorated to look like a VW-style surf bus
